name: University of Warwick
notes: Leading econometrics programme with strong City recruitment pipeline.

name: University of Bath
notes: Integrated placement year; high graduate employment in financial services.

name: University of Bristol
notes: Strong economic theory grounding; close links with finance industry employers.

name: University of Exeter
notes: Global economics specialism; strong research in environmental and behavioural economics.

name: University of Nottingham
notes: International economics focus; exchange opportunities across three global campuses.

name: University of Manchester
notes: Research-led economics with strong quantitative and policy-oriented modules.

name: University of Leeds
notes: Broad economics curriculum with dedicated finance and investment pathways.

name: Lancaster University
notes: AACSB-accredited business school; strong placement record in financial services.

name: Loughborough University
notes: Applied economics emphasis; excellent graduate employment across business sectors.

name: University of Southampton
notes: Quantitative-heavy programme; links to Southampton Business School research centres.

name: University of Birmingham
notes: Economics and finance joint honours popular; strong Midlands employer network.

name: University of Sheffield
notes: Applied economics focus; good public sector and NGO graduate destinations.

name: University of York
notes: Strong economics department with research in health and public economics.

name: Cardiff University
notes: Welsh economic policy expertise; growing links with Cardiff financial sector.

name: University of Liverpool
notes: Management School accredited; strong international student cohort and network.

name: Newcastle University
notes: Economics with placement year option; North East regional employer partnerships.

name: University of East Anglia
notes: Environmental and development economics strengths; research-active faculty.

name: Aston University
notes: Industry-focused economics; integrated placement year with Birmingham employers.

name: University of Sussex
notes: Progressive economics curriculum; strong international development research focus.

name: University of Reading
notes: Henley Business School; ICMA Centre provides global finance industry exposure.

name: University of Leicester
notes: Accessible entry; economics programme with flexible module choices.

name: University of Kent
notes: European economics focus; Brussels campus option for policy-oriented students.

name: University of Hull
notes: Smaller economics cohort; accessible entry with regional employer connections.
